assumptions: the first release should focus on one hero product
the fastest path should avoid forced account creation
guided scan prompts should stay short and visual
risks: too much text may reduce conversion
multi-product branching too early may confuse the user
next_required_validation: Hermes review before implementation in app UI
primary_user_goal: Help a first-time user understand the product quickly, complete a guided foot scan, receive a simple recommendation, and finish checkout with minimal friction.
friction_points: showing too many choices too early
ux_recommendation: Keep the first ZILFIT app experience focused on one hero product, one guided scan path, one recommendation path, and one fast checkout flow with minimal text and strong trust cues.
next_design_step: Turn the approved mobile flow into screen wireframes and UI copy for implementation.
